label: Hamas Rocket Barrage — Be'er Sheva
notes: Oct 7 opening barrage; ~3,000 rockets fired in first 24 hours toward multiple Israeli cities

label: Hamas Rockets — Ashkelon
notes: Oct 7 attack; Ashkelon is 15km from northern Gaza

label: Hamas Rockets — Tel Aviv Area
damage: Multiple impacts in greater Tel Aviv
notes: Oct 7; Hamas fired longest-range Ayyash rockets; Iron Dome largely effective

label: IDF Air Campaign — Gaza City (Oct 2023)
damage: Massive structural damage; northern Gaza largely destroyed by Nov 2023
notes: Israeli F-35I jets and F-16s conducted thousands of strikes; used 2,000-lb bombs in dense urban areas

label: IDF Ground Advance — Northern Gaza (Oct 2023)
notes: IDF 162nd Division and 36th Division entered from the north; cut off Gaza City by late October

label: IDF Ground Operation — Khan Yunis (Dec 2023)
damage: Large swaths of Khan Yunis destroyed
notes: Operation targeting Hamas's Khan Yunis brigade; IDF hunted Sinwar; operation lasted through April 2024

label: IDF Rafah Operation (May 2024)
damage: Rafah severely damaged; Philadelphi Corridor seized
notes: Despite US warnings, Israel advanced into Rafah. Rafah border crossing closed; ~1.4M displaced persons forced to move again

label: Aid Corridor — Kerem Shalom
notes: Main humanitarian aid route into Gaza; capacity severely limited. UN demanded 500+ trucks/day; actual throughput peaked ~200/day. Repeatedly closed by Israeli decisions and Hamas mortar attacks.

label: Hamas Breach — Be'eri Infiltration
notes: Hamas fighters breached fence and drove to kibbutzim; Be'eri was the bloodiest single community

label: Hamas Attack — Nova Music Festival
notes: Approximately 3,500 attendees at the trance music festival; Hamas gunmen attacked from multiple directions

label: IDF Operation — Jenin (2023-2025)
damage: Jenin camp partially destroyed; electrical/water infrastructure damaged
notes: IDF Operation Home and Garden (Jul 2023) — largest West Bank operation since 2002. Over 100 operations in Jenin/Tulkarm area through 2025.

label: PIJ / Hamas Rockets — Sderot
damage: Multiple structures hit; police station overrun on Oct 7
notes: Sderot is 1.5km from the Gaza fence; among the earliest-impacted Israeli communities

label: IDF Airstrikes — Rafah (May 2024)
notes: 26 May 2024 strike on humanitarian zone killed 45; US temporarily paused transfer of 2,000-lb bombs

label: Hamas Anti-Tank Fire — Northern Gaza
notes: Hamas employed RPGs, anti-tank guided missiles (Kornet), and IEDs against IDF Merkava tanks during urban operations

label: Israeli Navy — Coastal Bombardment
notes: Israeli Sa'ar 5 and Sa'ar 6 corvettes conducted offshore bombardments of Hamas maritime infrastructure and coastal targets

label: Egypt Aid Route — North Sinai to Rafah
notes: UN and NGO aid convoys via Al-Arish → Rafah. Egyptian military controlled access; entry into Gaza severely restricted. Egypt refused to absorb Palestinian refugees.

label: Hamas Long-Range Rocket — Haifa (2024)
notes: Hamas demonstrated longer-range capability firing towards Haifa during Oct 7 anniversary; most intercepted by Iron Dome

label: Al-Ahli Hospital Explosion (Oct 17, 2023)
notes: Major disputed event. US, Israeli, and multiple Western intelligence assessments concluded a misfired Palestinian Islamic Jihad rocket caused the explosion. Hamas and Arab media initially blamed an Israeli airstrike.

label: IDF Drone/Ground — Sinwar Elimination (Oct 16, 2024)
notes: Sinwar discovered by IDF ground patrol in Rafah during routine operations; engaged and killed. IDF drone also fired on him during the encounter. Confirmed by DNA testing.

label: Phase 1 Ceasefire Aid Surge (Jan–Mar 2025)
notes: During Phase 1 ceasefire, 600+ aid trucks per day entered Gaza. Humanitarian agencies reported partial easing of catastrophic conditions. Resumed operations in March 2025 again restricted aid.
